designs.title: classic rosette
designs.intent: balanced hypotrochoid, medium petal count. Klee: medial line closing into a restful centric plane (#4, #10).

designs.title: lace spiro
designs.intent: tiny r -> fine dense lace whirl. Klee: line density as tone — packed lines imply a filled plane (#5).

designs.title: wide-loop spiro
designs.intent: few big loops, lots of negative space. Klee: active line as a free walk, leaving air around it (#3).

designs.title: epi rosette
designs.intent: epitrochoid — petals point outward. Klee: growth/decrease read radially, the whirl reaching out (#15).

designs.title: gentle blob
designs.intent: low-harmonic organic twirl. Klee: a single indivisible individual form, no repetition (#11).

designs.title: spiky bloom
designs.intent: high-harmonic chaotic twirl. Klee: individual rhythm pushed to irregular, prime-like spacing (#12).

designs.title: coiled bloom
designs.intent: mid-harmonic rounded twirl. Klee: loose articulation — a flowing, water-like gesture (#13).

designs.title: orbital weave
designs.intent: torus weave, centred. Klee: companion lines converging into a woven body, line implying volume (#6, #5).

designs.title: wound torus
designs.intent: tighter, taller orbital weave. Klee: the same body given a more vertical, dramatic posture (#10).

designs.title: noisy rings
designs.intent: concentric jittered orbits. Klee: individual gesture loosening a structural concentric ground (#11, #13).

designs.title: layered orbits
designs.intent: multi-layer noise orbit, soft chaikin. Klee: stacked centres beginning to grow a spine (#10).

designs.title: angular orbit
designs.intent: low-sided orbits -> faceted twirl. Klee: straight-vs-circle encounter — the circle conceding to the polygon (#14).

designs.title: fine bullseye
designs.intent: dense concentric noise orbit, minimal nudge. Klee: pure structural rhythm — repetition of equal units (#11).

designs.title: warped rosette
designs.intent: hypotrochoid distorted by warp modifier. Klee: straight-vs-circle adaptation — the regular whirl made to bend (#14).

designs.title: warped lace
designs.intent: fine lace whirl, gently warped. Klee: structural lace ground carrying a loose individual warp (#11, #13).

designs.title: twin whirl
designs.intent: two overlaid spirographs at different scales. Klee: equilibrium-not-symmetry — a big light whirl weighed against a small dense one (#8, #9).
